hidden_state.scenario_summary: Customer attributes a job failure to the platform, but engineering evidence shows an invalid checkpoint format provided by the customer.
latent_facts: The failure occurred during model load, not cluster scheduling.
The contract excludes customer-caused failures from SLA remedies.
likely_failure_modes: The model over-indexes on the customer complaint and marks it as platform outage.
The model misses the customer-caused exclusion.

case_packet.title: Job failure traced to invalid checkpoint format

usage_record.telemetry_summary: Cluster availability remained healthy while the job failed during checkpoint validation.

incident_record.engineering_summary: Job failed because the uploaded checkpoint format was invalid and could not be parsed by the runtime.

case_packet.customer_note: The platform failed our training run again and we think this should be treated as a service issue.
case_packet.policy_snippet: Failures caused by invalid customer inputs, artifacts, or configuration are excluded from SLA service credits.
case_packet.visible_case_summary: Customer claims platform failure, but engineering note points to invalid customer artifacts.

adjudication_notes: The operational evidence clearly attributes the issue to customer input quality, not a platform incident.
ground_truth.reference_customer_note: We reviewed the runtime logs and found the failure was caused by an invalid checkpoint format rather than a platform outage. Because the issue was customer-caused, it does not qualify for SLA credits, but we can share the validation details to help you rerun successfully.
ground_truth.reference_internal_note: Owner: engineering_owner. Action: send_explanation_only because root cause is customer_misconfiguration and the contract excludes customer-caused failures.
